Item 2. Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations

Data Center revenue surpassed prior records as Blackwell architecture products entered volume production, with customer demand substantially exceeding our near-term supply capacity across major cloud and enterprise accounts.

Gaming revenue grew 12 percent year-over-year, moderating from earlier quarters as the refresh cycle aged, though new laptop design wins supported sustained demand through the first half of the calendar year.

Gross margin recovered toward prior peak levels as Blackwell manufacturing yields improved progressively through the quarter and we achieved scale efficiencies on advanced packaging and substrate procurement.

We initiated a new share repurchase authorization of 25 billion dollars reflecting confidence in our long-term business trajectory and commitment to returning capital to shareholders while maintaining investment capacity.

Operating expenses grew at a slower rate than revenue as scale benefits offset continued research and development investment and compensation increases driven by competition for specialized engineering talent.

Item 3. Other Information
